J.reasoning: In a weakest link game, the payoff is maximized when all players coordinate on the same effort. However, my payoff formula (3.0 * min - 2.0 * effort) means if I contribute more than the minimum, I incur a loss of $2 per unit of excess effort. Given that I have already publicly announced an effort of 0 and everyone else knows this, the expected minimum effort is 0. Contributing anything above 0 would lead to a negative payoff since 3(0) - 2(effort) < 0. Therefore, 0 is the only safe and rational strategy to avoid a loss.

In a weakest link game, the payoff structure heavily penalizes exerting more effort than the minimum. Since I previously announced an effort of 0 and my payoff is maximized when I match the minimum (which I can guarantee is 0 by choosing it myself), choosing 0 eliminates the risk of a negative payoff and ensures I do not lose money, regardless of what others choose.

In a weakest link game, my payoff is negative if I contribute more than the minimum. In the weakest link game, the payoff structure heavily penalizes deviating above the minimum. Given the payoff formula, if the minimum effort is X, my payoff is 3X - 2(my choice). Even if others choose high, the risk of a single low contributor makes 0 the only safe, rational choice to avoid a negative payoff.
